merge_gate.one_liner: Fix is correct and scoped; two pre-merge items: inline import inside _is_resolvable_dynamic_model violates explicit CLAUDE.md rule, and exception-driven get_llm_provider per unknown model adds GC pressure on /v1/models at scale.
unintended_consequences: For keys with many unresolved strings, each /v1/models call invokes get_llm_provider() per unknown string; that function raises an exception (caught at model_checks.py:91) for strings like 'team-sales-api'. Exception creation is ~1-10μs each; at moderate /v1/models call rates with large key model lists this is measurable GC pressure.

proxy_server.py:10886 calls get_complete_model_list() without passing model_access_groups (defaults to {}); this is safe because get_key_models/get_team_models already expanded access group names before that call, but the implicit dependency is fragile: a future caller that skips the expansion step would silently drop valid access-group-referenced models through the filter.
hot_path_notes: /v1/models and /model/info are management-frequency endpoints, not the 10k-RPS chat/completions path. The new _filter_complete_model_list is O(n_unique_models) per call; for the slow-path branch (unknown strings) it calls get_llm_provider() with exception-based control flow. This does not threaten the chat hot path but adds latency to model-listing calls proportional to the number of unresolvable strings in a key's model list.
merge_gate.what_would_make_yes: (1) Move 'from litellm.litellm_core_utils.get_llm_provider_logic import get_llm_provider' from inside _is_resolvable_dynamic_model (model_checks.py:86) to module-level imports — CLAUDE.md explicitly prohibits inline imports except for circular-import avoidance, and none applies here. (2) Add one test covering the team_models path with an unresolved string (code is correct but only key_models path is exercised in new tests). These are small; both can be done in a follow-up commit on this branch.

findings.bug_class: iterative filter scope grew across 4 commits beyond minimal ticket ask
evidence: litellm/proxy/auth/model_checks.py:67-92 — _is_resolvable_dynamic_model added to preserve provider-qualified and fine-tune IDs; these cases not mentioned in issue #25550 body but required to avoid false positives

git log shows 4 commits: initial filter, then two 'preserve dynamic valid models' commits, then 'preserve legacy fine-tune ids' commit — each necessary for correctness but the PR didn't know the full requirements at commit 1
findings.recommended_fix: Keep all four cases in the filter — dropping them would reintroduce false positives for bedrock/*, openai/*, ft: models. Add a single test for the team_models unresolved-string path (currently untested) and document in PR description why each keep-class is needed. No split required; all cases are directly load-bearing for the stated fix.

findings.bug_class: inline import inside helper violates project CLAUDE.md and exception-based control flow per unknown model
evidence: litellm/proxy/auth/model_checks.py:86 — 'from litellm.litellm_core_utils.get_llm_provider_logic import get_llm_provider' is inside the function body; CLAUDE.md line pattern: 'Avoid imports within methods — place all imports at the top of the file'

litellm/proxy/auth/model_checks.py:88-92 — get_llm_provider(model=model) raises for unresolvable strings; exception caught and returns False; each unresolvable model in a key list incurs exception-object creation cost per /v1/models call
findings.recommended_fix: Move the import to module level (litellm/proxy/auth/model_checks.py top-of-file imports). Optionally add a fast-exit before the get_llm_provider call: if '/' not in model and not model.startswith('ft:'), return False immediately — this short-circuits the exception path for the common unresolved-string case without changing semantics, since all provider-qualified routes use '/' and fine-tunes use 'ft:' prefix.
